names.scientific_name: Monopotassium phosphate (E340i), Dipotassium phosphate (E340ii), Tripotassium phosphate (E340iii)

natural_occurrence.description: Potassium phosphates do not occur naturally in free form. While phosphate compounds are essential components of all living organisms, the potassium salts used in food are synthetically manufactured.

manufacturing.description: Produced by neutralizing phosphoric acid with potassium hydroxide or potassium carbonate. The degree of neutralization determines which form is produced: monopotassium phosphate (E340i, KH2PO4), dipotassium phosphate (E340ii, K2HPO4), or tripotassium phosphate (E340iii, K3PO4). Each form has different pH and buffering properties.

other_uses.cosmetics: Buffering agent in personal care formulations.
other_uses.industrial: Fertilizers (especially monopotassium phosphate), detergents, water treatment.
other_uses.pharmaceutical: Buffering agent, electrolyte replenisher, urinary acidifier (monopotassium phosphate).
other_uses.household: Some cleaning products and water softeners.

eu.conditions: Quantum satis in many categories; specific limits in others due to concerns about total dietary phosphate intake.

usa.notes: Often used as a reduced-sodium alternative to sodium phosphates

safety_evaluations.jecfa_adi: 0-70 mg/kg bw/day (as phosphorus, for all phosphates)
safety_evaluations.efsa_adi: 40 mg/kg bw/day (as phosphorus, for all phosphates)

regulatory_history.event: EFSA's comprehensive evaluation of food phosphates expressed concern about high dietary intake from additives, particularly in Western processed food diets. Applies to all phosphate additives including potassium phosphates.

fun_facts: Often used as a sodium-free alternative to sodium phosphates in low-sodium products
Provides potassium supplementation while also serving as a buffering agent
Monopotassium phosphate is widely used as a highly efficient fertilizer in agriculture
Different forms have different buffering capacities - dipotassium phosphate is commonly used in baking powder
Part of the growing trend toward reducing sodium in processed foods while maintaining functionality
